title: How to get a police certificate - Mongolia - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate - Mongolia
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.
Do I need to provide a Police Certificate with my Application for Permanent Residence? Yes
Name of document(s) to get: Certificate/Reference Letter of No Criminal Convictions

## How to get a Police Certificate:

### If you are a citizen of Mongolia:

#### If you live in Mongolia:
You can apply at your local police authority

#### If you live outside of Mongolia:
You can nominate a representative to act on your behalf in Mongolia. To nominate a representative, you must give the person specific written authorization and your original Mongolian identification card. The person acting on your behalf must then present the documents to the local police authorities.

### If you are not a citizen of Mongolia:

#### If you live in Mongolia:
You can apply at your local police authority

#### If you live outside of Mongolia:
You can nominate a representative to act on your behalf in Mongolia. To nominate a representative, you must give the person specific written authorization and your original passport. The person acting on your behalf must then present the documents to the local police authorities.

### Information and documents you need:
- Your original Mongolian identification card (If you are a Non-Mongolian national, your original passport)

### Special notes and considerations:
The original police certificate must be submitted along with your application for permanent residence, and must be accompanied by an English or French translation prepared by an accredited translator
